title: [lichen_l2.c:580] last_seen update on match creates timing side-channel
description:
What: peer_try_all_pubkeys() updates peer_table[found_idx].last_seen = k_uptime_get() on finding a match. This happens after the constant-time iteration completes, so it doesn't leak the match index via iteration timing.

However, the k_uptime_get() call and peer_table write DO happen only on success. If an attacker can measure total function execution time, they can distinguish 'matched some peer' from 'matched no peer' - but this information is also leaked by the return value (-LICHEN_EAUTH vs 0).

Not a bug - the timing side-channel comment (lines 519-535) addresses iteration order, and the final update doesn't leak WHICH peer matched.

Closing as not-a-bug.
close_reason: Not a bug - the timing difference only reveals success/failure which is already conveyed by return value.
